message:
feat(algorithms): native single-target + all-pairs directed shortest paths

Extends fnx-algorithms with three directed-graph shortest-path
routines that previously had to fall back to the Python/NetworkX
surface:

 - single_target_shortest_path_directed: reverse-BFS from a target
   node over in-edges (predecessor map walks backwards from target
   to every reachable source). Handles arbitrary node types via the
   generic node hash parameterization already used by the undirected
   variant.
 - single_target_shortest_path_length_directed: same traversal,
   returns length-only map. Lets callers skip the full path
   reconstruction when they just need the distance matrix.
 - all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length_directed: runs per-source directed
   dijkstra with optional cutoff; reuses the existing binary-heap
   implementation but swaps in-neighbors for out-neighbors at each
   relaxation step.

crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs wires in a new helper
`accumulate_weighted_average_shortest_path_length` that feeds the
directed all-pairs result into the average-path-length formula
(sum of distances over n*(n-1)) with proper handling for
disconnected components.

Closes the last Rust-side gap for the directed shortest-path family
that was blocking additional PY_WRAPPER -> RUST_NATIVE promotions.
